channel.title: Cancer - Skin on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Cancer - Skin on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Dermatology's Disastrous War Against The Sun
item.description:
• Article by A MidWesternDoctor.com

Article by A MidWesternDoctor.com

APR 21, 2024

Story at a Glance:

Skin cancers are by far the most commonly diagnosed cancer in the United States, so to prevent them, the public is constantly told to avoid the sun. However, while the relatively benign skin cancers are caused by sun exposure, the ones responsible for most skin cancer deaths are due to a lack of sunlight.

This is unfortunate because sunlight is arguably the most important nutrient for the human body, as avoiding it doubles one’s rate of dying and significantly increases their risk of cancer.

item.title: Melanoma risk reduced by Vitamin D and other nutrients - many studies
item.description:
• Nutrient-Based Approaches for Melanoma: Prevention and Therapeutic Insights

Nutrient-Based Approaches for Melanoma: Prevention and Therapeutic Insights

Nutrients 2023, 15(20), 4483; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu15204483

by Yucheng Dong 1,†,Jiaxin Wei 2,†,Fan Yang 3,Yang Qu 4,Jiuzuo Huang 1,* andDi Shi 2,*

Melanoma, a prevalent and lethal form of skin cancer, remains a formidable challenge in terms of prevention and treatment. While significant progress has been made in understanding its pathogenesis and treatment, the quest for effective prevention strategies and therapeutic approaches remains ongoing. Considering the increased advancements in understanding the dynamic interplay between nutrients and melanoma, we aim to offer a refreshed perspective on nutrient-based approaches for melanoma prevention and adjunctive therapy. In contrast to other studies, we have innovatively provided a detailed exposition of the nutrients’ influences on melanoma prognosis and treatment.

item.title: Metastatic Melanoma survived 2X longer if adequate level of Vitamin D
item.description:
• From: CureToday July 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - Metastatic Cancer probably reduced by vitamin D - many studies

From: CureToday July 2023

  • “Among the study’s 200 participants with inoperable, locally advanced or metastatic melanoma who all received anti-PD-1 immunotherapy via Opdivo (nivolumab) or Keytruda (pembrolizumab), the objective response rate (ORR) among patients with low vitamin D levels which were not supplemented was 36.2%_ , while the response rate was 56%__ for patients with either normal baseline levels of vitamin D or those who obtained normal levels via supplementation, according to the study.”

item.title: Melanoma death risk 0.63 X lower if poor Vitamin D Binding Gene
item.description:
• Association of functional, inherited vitamin D-binding protein variants with melanoma-specific death
      • Note: Other studies have found an INCREASE in cancer problems with Vitamin D Binding Protein
      • See also: Melanoma might be suppressed by Vitamin D - many studies

Association of functional, inherited vitamin D-binding protein variants with melanoma-specific death

JNCI Cancer Spectr . 2023 Jul 26;pkad051. doi: 10.1093/jncics/pkad051

David Corley Gibbs 1, Nancy E Thomas 2 3, Peter A Kanetsky 4, Li Luo 5, Klaus J Busam 6, Anne E Cust 7 8 9, Hoda Anton-Culver 10, Richard P Gallagher 11, Roberto Zanetti 12 13, Stefano Rosso 12 13, Lidia Sacchetto 12 13, Sharon N Edmiston 3, Kathleen Conway 3 14, David W Ollila 3 15, Colin B Begg 6, Marianne Berwick 5, Sarah V Ward 6 16, Irene Orlow 6

item.title: Melanoma is 1.4X more-likely if low Vitamin D – meta-analysis
item.description:
• Vitamin D insufficiency and serum levels related to the incidence and stage of cutaneous melanoma: a systematic review and meta-analysis
      • VitaminDWiki - Melanoma might be suppressed by Vitamin D - many studies

Vitamin D insufficiency and serum levels related to the incidence and stage of cutaneous melanoma: a systematic review and meta-analysis

Melanoma Res . 2023 May 18. doi: 10.1097/CMR.0000000000000897 PDF is behind a $51 paywall

Richard A Shellenberger 1, Sunaina Gowda 1, Heidi Kurn 1, Jeremy Albright 2, MacKenzie H Mayo 1

item.title: Cancers reduced by Vitamin D (Skin cancer in this review)
item.description:
• The Chemoprotective Role of Vitamin D in Skin Cancer: A Systematic Review
      • See also VitaminDWiki

The Chemoprotective Role of Vitamin D in Skin Cancer: A Systematic Review

Cancer Management and ResearchVol 2022:14 Pages 3551—3565 DOI https://doi.org/10.2147/CMAR.S389591

Sutedja EK , Arianto TR , Lesmana R, Suwarsa O , Setiabudiawan B

  • 1Department of Dermatology and Venereology, Faculty of Medicine Universitas Padjadjaran – Dr. Hasan Sadikin Hospital, Bandung, West Java, Indonesia;

  • 2Department of Physiology, Faculty of Medicine Universitas Padjadjaran – Dr. Hasan Sadikin Hospital Bandung, Bandung, West Java, Indonesia;

item.title: Melanoma Cancer detected more often (perhaps due to infrequent intense UVA)
item.description:
• Cutaneous Melanoma: Sheep in Wolves Clothing?
      • See also Overview Suntan, melanoma and vitamin D
      • People who get UVA daily rarely get melanoma

Cutaneous Melanoma: Sheep in Wolves Clothing?

Anticancer Research 42: 5021-5025 (2022) doi:10.21873/anticanres.16010

FRANK R. DE GRUIJL1 and BRUCE K. ARMSTRONG2

1 dermatology, Leiden University Medical Center, Leiden, the Netherlands;

2 School of Global and Population Health, The University of Western Australia, Perth, WA, Australia

item.title: Photodynamic Therapy (PDT) aided by various forms and types of Vitamin D (1.06 X to 10 X)
item.description:
• Vitamin D and Vitamin D3 Supplementation during Photodynamic Therapy: A Review
      • See related in VitaminDWiki

Vitamin D and Vitamin D3 Supplementation during Photodynamic Therapy: A Review

Nutrients 2022, 14(18), 3805; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu14183805

Anna Mazur, Katarzyna Koziorowska, Klaudia Dynarowicz, David Aebisher. Dorota Bartusik-Aebisher

Photodynamic therapy is an unconventional yet increasingly common method of treating dermatological diseases and cancer that is implemented more often in adults than in children. Current clinical uses include treatment of actinic keratosis, superficial basal cell carcinomas, and acne. Despite its high efficiency, photodynamic therapy support supplements have recently been reported in the literature, including calcitriol (1,25-dihydroxycholecalciferol), the active form of vitamin D, and vitamin D3 cholecalciferol.

item.title: Melanoma might be suppressed by Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• Melanoma thickness inversly associated with Vitamin D - meta-analysis Aug 2022
      • PTEN: a novel target for vitamin D in melanoma - Aug 2022
      • VitaminDWiki - other references

Melanoma thickness inversly associated with Vitamin D - meta-analysis Aug 2022

To identify the association between dietary vitamin D intake and serum levels and risk or prognostic factors for melanoma-systematic review and meta-analysis

BMJ Open 2022;12:e052442. doi: 10.1136/bmjopen-2021-052442

Yadong Song1, Hongyan Lu2, Yan Cheng1 GROzdyfy@163.com

item.title: Vitamin D from the sun reduces the risk of internal cancers, but increases some skin cancers
Melanoma associated with vitamin D less than 30 ng (9X)
Book: Sunlight, UV, Vitamin D and Receptor, Skin and other Cancers
item.description:
Sunlight, Vitamin D and Skin Cancer Third Edition - 2020

Download from sci-hub via VitaminDWiki

Table of Contents

item.title: 10X more skin cancer cells killed by photodynamic therapy when add Vitamin D
Sunlight on skin has decreased 9X while melanoma has increased 30X
item.description:
Post by Marc Sorenson on Facebook

Squamous cell carcinoma is not one of the more dangerous skin cancers. That dubious distinction goes to melanoma, which can be deadly. When you say that the number-one way to prevent skin cancer is to avoid sun exposure, you are not correct.

Here are a few facts about melanomna:

  • •In the past four decades, sunscreen use has increased by 400%, and melanoma has increased by 400%!

item.title: Melanoma 2.1 X more likely if low vitamin D – meta-analysis
item.description:
• The Association Between Serum Vitamin D Level and Risk and Prognosis of Melanoma: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

The Association Between Serum Vitamin D Level and Risk and Prognosis of Melanoma: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

J Eur Acad Dermatol Venereol, 2020 Jan 9, DOI: 10.1111/jdv.16189

Tsung-Yu Tsai 1, Che-Yuan Kuo 2, Yu-Chen Huang 1 3

Background: Vitamin D has antineoplastic effects, but the synthesis of vitamin D requires ultraviolet radiation, a known risk factor for melanoma.

item.title: Melanoma cancer growth slowed by increased Vitamin D Receptor (yet again)
item.description:
• Vitamin D-VDR signaling inhibits Wnt/beta-catenin-mediated melanoma progression and promotes anti-tumor immunity
      • I suspect that increased Vitamin D and Increased Vitamin D Receptor will PREVENT as well as TREAT many Cancers - Founder of VitaminDWiki Oct 2019

Vitamin D-VDR signaling inhibits Wnt/beta-catenin-mediated melanoma progression and promotes anti-tumor immunity

Cancer Research DOI: 10.1158/0008-5472.CAN-18-3927

The risk of 44 diseases at least double with poor Vitamin D Receptor as of Oct 2019

I suspect that increased Vitamin D and Increased Vitamin D Receptor will PREVENT as well as TREAT many Cancers - Founder of VitaminDWiki Oct 2019

Vitamin D makes melanoma cells less aggressive Medical News report on this study

item.title: High levels of Vitamin D and low rates of skin cancer among commercial fishermen
item.description:
Sun exposure, skin lesions and vitamin D production: evaluation in a population of fishermen.

An Bras Dermatol. 2019 Jul 29;94(3):279-286. doi: 10.1590/abd1806-4841.20197201.

Coutinho RCS1,2, Santos AFD2,3,4, Costa JGD3,5, Vanderlei AD2,6.

88% of the fishermen at 10 degrees latitude had 30+ ng/ml of Vitamin D

  • Most S. Calif lifeguards have good level of vitamin D (above 40 ng) - Aug 2016

item.title: Basal Cell Carcinoma reduced when Vitamin D levels kept above 25 ng
The Risk of Skin Cancer From Sun Exposure Is Overblown
item.description:
That’s the gist of what the last 15,950 studies on vitamin D have added to our understanding. Unless something particularly groundbreaking comes out, you can expect the next update in 2021. If you missed the first five videos in this series, see:

  • Do Vitamin D Supplements Help with Diabetes, Weight Loss, and Blood Pressure?

  • Will You Live Longer If You Take Vitamin D Supplements?

  • How Much Vitamin D Should You Take?

item.title: Increased incidence of 7 cancers if live far from equator (if not take vitamin D)
item.description:
The Role of Vitamin D and Sunlight Incidence in Cancer.

Anticancer Agents Med Chem. 2019 Mar 12. doi: 10.2174/1389557519666190312123212

Câmara AB1, Brandãob IA2.

  • 1 Department of Biophysics and Pharmacology, Bioscience Center, Federal University of Rio Grande do Norte, 59064-741, Natal/RN. Brazil.

  • 2 Metrópole Digital Institute, Federal University of Rio Grande do Norte, 59078-970, Natal/RN. Brazil.

#### Cancers

  • Getting little UV is associated with 15 types of Cancer – Jan 2012

item.title: Rates of some newly diagnosed Cancers increased with higher Vitamin D (217,000 Danes)
Melanoma Is a Disease of Office Workers
item.description:
Melanoma Is a Disease of Office Workers Mercola - Dec 2018

  • “Interestingly, melanoma appears on body parts that don’t usually see much sun but which occasionally get burned, such as men’s torsos or the back of women’s legs.5 In one population-based, case-controlled study, researchers found a pattern of significantly increased risk of melanoma for lesions on the trunk of individuals who reported using sunscreen to enable them to spend more time sunbathing.

item.title: The Greatest Public Health Mistake of the 20th Century (sunscreen block Vitamin D) - 2017
item.description:
Mercola May 2017

  • “The recommendation to avoid sun exposure and always use sunblock when outdoors may be the greatest public health mistake of the 20th century

  • “Previous research has found vitamin D may prevent 30 deaths for each death caused by skin cancer”

  • “Dermatologists Make No Allowances for Skin Color”

  • “Sunscreens May Sabotage Health in More Ways Than One”

“Only two of 10 melanoma studies found that sunscreen was protective against this condition; three found no association either way. None found sunscreen use protected against basal cell carcinoma

item.title: Better Cancer survival if higher vitamin D a decade earlier (esp. Melanoma, Kidney, Prostate)
